items.briefing:
A ransomware group known as "The Gentlemen" has topped the charts for ransomware activity in Q2 2026, serving as a reminder that ransomware remains a serious threat to Australian organisations including RTOs. Separately, a large-scale phishing campaign has been discovered where attackers hide invisible text in emails to trick AI-based security filters into missing malicious content — meaning some harmful emails may slip through your organisation's defences undetected.

Multiple security experts are also highlighting that AI security tools require stronger human oversight, as they can make mistakes. **Practical tip:** Don't rely solely on automated email filters — always pause and verify unexpected emails requesting actions, links, or attachments before clicking.

featured.title: DigiCert survey finds 81% of Australian organisations hit by AI security incidents or vulnerabilities
featured.quote: 81% of Australian organisations already experiencing AI security incidents signals urgent need for defensive strategy.
